artifacts.content:
Hi Em,

I’m hiding in the break room for five minutes before the 3 p.m. controls call and wanted to write this down while it’s fresh.

I just got off the phone with the facilities guy at Harborview Medical Center about Patient Room 7A. I pushed for them to modify the Olivio fixtures over Sophie’s bed so their in‑house biomedical engineer can re‑aim them on site. Her pediatric oncologist at Seattle Children’s was pretty clear about keeping light levels under 150 lux at bed height during the chemo cycles starting in October 2025, and the stock hardware was going to make that a pain. It felt weird talking about “Patient Room 7A” and realizing that’s *our* kid’s room.

On the work side, I finally locked the spec today for the Harborview Promenade Plaza Lighting Upgrade: eighteen Olivio Grande RGBW universal‑mount luminaires, medium 50° optics, semi‑matte black, DMX scenes for Evening Ambient, Event Color Wash, Maintenance White, and Holiday Mode. I actually typed the product modification line as “Factory aim and lock each luminaire at 25° tilt and 15° rotation from mounting reference, per attached aiming diagram HVDG‑PLZ‑AIM‑01.” Felt like such a nerdy victory.

Tonight, if you’re home on time from Seattle Children’s, I promised Sophie we’d do the unicorn‑city‑with‑lights thing again with the little RGBW demo in the living room. Lucas keeps trying to grab it, so I might tape it to the tripod.

Text me when you get a break. How are your kids on the floor today?

Love,
Daniel

Mom,

I’ve been going back and forth on how to tell you this without making you cry at your desk, but you’d probably rather know the details than have me keep it vague.

For the Peter Hsu Reflection Garden at Bellevue Botanical Garden, I asked the manufacturer to engrave “In memory of Peter Hsu (1955–2024)” on the pole access covers for the Olivio fixtures by the memorial bench. They’re also going to adjust the factory tilt so the heads softly graze the stone marker where we scattered Dad’s ashes last March 2024. At night it should feel like a quiet lantern glow around that corner, not a spotlight.

The funny part is how much of my work life is wrapped up in Dad things right now. At Harborview Promenade Plaza, I’m still wrangling the DMX scenes—Evening Ambient, Event Color Wash, Maintenance White, Holiday Mode—and I keep thinking about that soft pink lantern effect we improvised at his memorial service on 2024‑03‑09 down at the plaza. We had to override the four standard scenes to get that color you liked. I’m actually asking the controls rep if we can store a special fifth DMX scene that mimics that look on the Line 2 fixtures so we can bring it back for anniversaries.

I’ll let you know when the garden aiming mock‑up is scheduled so you can come with me if you feel up to the walk.

Love,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Final lighting details for Dad’s garden

artifacts.content:
Personal risk + family health notes – DO NOT FORGET

1) Mom’s vertigo / office incident
- January 2025: Mom (Linda) had that vertigo episode and fall during her visit to the Harborview Design Group open studio. Trigger was a mis‑aimed track head that someone tilted way above my intended angle.
- Afterward, our landlord’s lawyer pushed for proof that anything new we install can’t be tipped up into people’s eyes.
- Result: for all new Olivio heads over the open studio, I’m insisting on a non‑standard lockable tilt mechanism so they physically cannot be field‑tilted above 25°. Need photos of the locks for the lease file and for Mom’s peace of mind next time she visits.

2) Lucas’s sleep / courtyard spill
- Spring 2025: pediatric sleep specialist at Swedish Medical Center stressed keeping exterior light spill into the kids’ bedroom as low as possible during his insomnia treatment.
- Shared courtyard at 214 Harborview Terrace is the main culprit. I’m working with the HOA to modify the Olivio poles’ drivers so we can schedule dimming down to 5% between 11:00 p.m. and 4:00 a.m. That window seemed to matter most to the specialist.

3) Sophie’s chemo lighting
- For Patient Room 7A at Harborview Medical Center, make sure the fixtures really can be re‑aimed on site so we can stay under 150 lux at bed height during October 2025 chemo cycles.

These all blur together: work specs, landlord/legal, and my own family’s health. Remember why the details matter when I’m tempted to cut corners.

Hi Kevin,

Catching you up on a few lighting‑and‑family crossovers before we all get buried in Q1 deadlines.

First, thank you again for handling those quarterly checks at the Harborview Promenade Plaza canopy. I still feel awful about 2025‑02‑14, when you got that minor electrical burn at the service hatch because Line 2 was running such a dim ambient scene. I’ve asked the controls team to add a password‑protected service scene that forces the fixtures to full‑output 4000K white for at least 3 hours whenever you’re up there, so you’re not poking around in half‑dark anymore.

On a different front, remember Mom’s fall on the back steps in Bellevue and her short rehab stay at Swedish Medical Center last summer? In July 2024, when Carlos Ramirez put in that temporary outdoor junction box setup behind her house, we had him use a 10 ft UV‑resistant black whip with a pre‑terminated quick‑disconnect (no plug, just hardwire pigtails). It worked really well for her confidence at night. I’ve started copying that detail into my specs—same 10 ft factory‑installed UV‑resistant whip for outdoor junction boxes—so we’re not dealing with sketchy plug‑in cord caps anymore.

Meanwhile, the plaza itself is finally getting real: eighteen Olivio Grande RGBW universal mounts, medium 50° optics, semi‑matte black, all the DMX scenes (Evening Ambient, Event Color Wash, Maintenance White, Holiday Mode). Once they’re up, I want you there for the first service‑scene test so we can confirm it’s bright enough for your inspections.

Tell Grace and the kids we owe you a Bainbridge ferry day once the weather stops pretending it’s November.

– Daniel

metadata.subject: Service scene for your inspections + Mom’s backyard setup

artifacts.content:
Event: Promenade safety + resident complaints debrief
Time: 2025-09-02, 2:00–3:30 p.m.
Location: Harborview Design Group conference room, Seattle, WA
Attendees: Daniel Hsu, managing principal, PM team, controls rep (Teams)

Agenda / Notes:

1) Seattle Fire Marshal temporary safety order (August 2025)
- After the crowd‑control incident during the Harborview Promenade Plaza concert, where a strobe‑like effect from our color‑changing sequence was blamed for triggering a patron’s seizure, Fire Marshal’s Office issued a temporary order.
- Need to pre‑program an additional DMX scene on Line 2 that locks fixtures at 100% neutral white for at least 6 continuous hours (no color chasing, no strobe characteristics) for events flagged as high‑risk.

2) Holiday Mode complaints from Harborview Towers
- November 2024: multiple written complaints from residents about the red/green chasing pattern in Holiday Mode running late on school nights.
- One letter specifically from unit 7C (across the hall from my unit 7B) said their kids couldn’t sleep with the color wash bouncing off bedroom walls.
- Action: add a hard lockout so Holiday Mode cannot be activated after 9:00 p.m. Sunday–Thursday. Confirm with condo board and HOA.

3) NDA / conference room lighting near‑miss (October 2024)
- Subcontractor almost walked out of the Harborview Design Group conference room during a dimmed presentation with unsigned client NDA packets and Selux pricing sheets plus a competitor’s quote still on the table.
- Perkins Coie attorney advised adding a special “NDA signing” emergency scene that snaps table to full light.

Follow‑up: Update plaza lighting narrative and controls matrix; brief Priya.

Hi Mark,

Quick update from my side on the lighting/insurance saga at 214 Harborview Terrace.

After the October 2024 electrical fire in our stack, I did that mockup in early November on the rooftop terrace at 214 Harborview Terrace, Apt 7B so the electrician and the homeowners insurance adjuster could see a safer approach. I used a 10 ft UV‑resistant black whip with a pre‑terminated quick‑disconnect suitable for outdoor junction boxes (no plug, just hardwire pigtails) to match what we’re now pushing for in the permanent repair. I sent Dana some photos, but I’m happy to resend if they got buried in the claim thread.

That same whip detail also showed up in the August 5, 2024 change order I wrote for the Harborview Design Group office terrace at 214 Harborview. After Megan Lawson, your lawyer, warned that our plug‑in string lights could be used against us in lease renewal negotiations, we shifted the terrace fixtures to a 10 ft factory‑installed UV‑resistant whip with quick‑disconnects so everything is cleanly hardwired and code‑friendly.

I’m now standardizing that spec on my professional projects too—Lake Union Lofts, the Harborview Promenade Plaza Lighting Upgrade, etc.—so we’re not mixing exposed cord caps and questionable plug strips with outdoor junction boxes.

On a personal note, our kids are finally sleeping better now that the temporary corridor fixtures aren’t flickering. Once the insurance company signs off on the full repair scope, I’d love to sit down with you and the HOA board to talk about the courtyard lighting dimming schedule as well.

Thanks for staying on top of this.

Best,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Rooftop whip mockup + terrace lighting compliance

artifacts.content:
Evening brain dump – lighting + family (write before I forget)

1) Sophie’s hospital room
- Need to check in with the team at Harborview Medical Center about Patient Room 7A before October 2025. We asked them to modify the Olivio fixtures so their in‑house biomedical engineer can re‑aim them, per Sophie’s pediatric oncologist at Seattle Children’s. Goal: keep light levels below 150 lux at bed height during chemo cycles. Bring the RGBW demo to show her what “soft” can still look like.

2) Promenade memorial + Fire Marshal
- For Dad’s memorial at Harborview Promenade Plaza on 2024‑03‑09, we overrode the standard four DMX scenes (Evening Ambient, Event Color Wash, Maintenance White, Holiday Mode) to get that soft pink lantern effect Mom loved. I’ve asked the controls rep to add a special fifth DMX scene on Line 2 that mimics that look.
- Separate but related: after the August 2025 concert incident where a strobe‑like color change was blamed for a patron’s seizure, Seattle Fire Marshal’s Office wants an additional safety scene: 100% neutral white for at least 6 continuous hours, no flashy transitions.

3) Office + NDA panic
- Don’t forget to finalize the “NDA signing” emergency scene in the Harborview Design Group conference room. Perkins Coie is right: we can’t have another October 2024 situation where a subcontractor almost walks out during a dimmed presentation with unsigned NDAs, Selux pricing sheets, and a competitor quote still on the table.

4) Home
- Ask HOA next meeting about codifying the 11 p.m.–4 a.m. courtyard dimming to help Lucas’s sleep.

Notes to self:

- Date: 2026-01-22
- Fixture Order Code Olgr: OLGR-U-L50-RGBW-M50-BL-UNV
- Light Engine L50 50W Max: true
- Voltage Unv 120V 277V: true
